briefing.headline: 10 high-priority items in the latest EM scan
briefing.summary: Top sources today: SJTREM, JACEP Open, EMJ. Main themes: guideline or formal recommendation, resuscitation care, reviews. Best early reads: Civilian penetrating traumatic brain injury in South-East Norway; Video Review of Cardiac Arrest: A Scoping Review.
briefing.editorialSummary:
Despite high mortality in cpTBI, most survivors achieved favourable outcome. We found a lower-than-expected guideline compliance for prophylactic AB and ASM, ICP monitoring, and surgical revision within 12 h. Thus, we identified several key factors that can improve cpTBI treatment at our institution.

Video review enables a precise, multidomain assessment of resuscitation performance of CA that conventional data sources cannot provide. Future work should prioritize consensus definitions and establishing minimum reporting standards.

Acute hyperkalaemia is a potentially life-threatening electrolyte disturbance frequently encountered in emergency departments. Timely recognition and appropriate treatment are critical to prevent serious complications such as cardiac arrhythmias and death. This narrative review summarises current evidence-based and guideline-based recommendations for the emergency management of hyperkalaemia, with a focus on practical challenges and frequently encountered clinical uncertainties.

dailyEditorial.title: Civilian penetrating traumatic brain injury in South-East Norway
dailyEditorial.takeaway: We found a lower-than-expected guideline compliance for prophylactic AB and ASM, ICP monitoring, and surgical revision within 12 h. Thus, we identified several key factors that can improve cpTBI treatment at our institution.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Despite high mortality in cpTBI, most survivors achieved favourable outcome.
dailyEditorial.summary: Despite high mortality in cpTBI, most survivors achieved favourable outcome. We found a lower-than-expected guideline compliance for prophylactic AB and ASM, ICP monitoring, and surgical revision within 12 h. Thus, we identified several key factors that can improve cpTBI treatment at our institution.

dailyEditorial.title: Video Review of Cardiac Arrest: A Scoping Review
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Future work should prioritize consensus definitions and establishing minimum reporting standards. Video review enables a precise, multidomain assessment of resuscitation performance of CA that conventional data sources cannot provide.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Video review enables a precise, multidomain assessment of resuscitation performance of CA that conventional data sources cannot provide.
dailyEditorial.summary: Video review enables a precise, multidomain assessment of resuscitation performance of CA that conventional data sources cannot provide. Future work should prioritize consensus definitions and establishing minimum reporting standards.

dailyEditorial.title: Acute hyperkalaemia in emergency care: evidence-based approaches
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Timely recognition and appropriate treatment are critical to prevent serious complications such as cardiac arrhythmias and death. This narrative review summarises current evidence-based and guideline-based recommendations for the emergency management of hyperkalaemia, with a focus on practical challenges and frequently encountered clinical uncertainties.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Acute hyperkalaemia is a potentially life-threatening electrolyte disturbance frequently encountered in emergency departments.
dailyEditorial.summary: Acute hyperkalaemia is a potentially life-threatening electrolyte disturbance frequently encountered in emergency departments. Timely recognition and appropriate treatment are critical to prevent serious complications such as cardiac arrhythmias and death. This narrative review summarises current evidence-based and guideline-based recommendations for the emergency management of hyperkalaemia, with a focus on practical challenges and frequently encountered clinical uncertainties.

dailyEditorial.title: Clinical decision rules for obtaining chest radiography in adult patients presenting to the emergency department with non-traumatic chest pain: a systematic review and meta-analysis
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Radiography is used in 70% of cases but identifies clinically significant findings in only 1.5%–2.1%. The predominance of non-actionable imaging results, combined with paucity of decision rules, prompted this systematic review to inform the development of a new clinical decision rule (CDR).
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Introduction Chest pain is the second leading emergency department (ED) presentation, with its associated diagnostics requiring ED resource utilisation.
dailyEditorial.summary: Introduction Chest pain is the second leading emergency department (ED) presentation, with its associated diagnostics requiring ED resource utilisation. Radiography is used in 70% of cases but identifies clinically significant findings in only 1.5%–2.1%. The predominance of non-actionable imaging results, combined with paucity of decision rules, prompted this systematic review to inform the development of a new clinical decision rule (CDR).

dailyEditorial.title: Management of patients with suspected but unidentified poisoning in the emergency department: a joint Royal College of Emergency Medicine and National Poisons Information Service best practice guideline
dailyEditorial.takeaway: The Royal College of Emergency Medicine and National Poisons Information Service have produced this guidance to provide a generalised clinical approach to any poisoned patient in the emergency department. This guideline provides a clinical approach to support the initial assessment of a patient, identification of potential toxic agents and emergency management.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: The management of patients suspected, but not confirmed, as being poisoned is challenging.
dailyEditorial.summary: The management of patients suspected, but not confirmed, as being poisoned is challenging. The Royal College of Emergency Medicine and National Poisons Information Service have produced this guidance to provide a generalised clinical approach to any poisoned patient in the emergency department. This guideline provides a clinical approach to support the initial assessment of a patient, identification of potential toxic agents and emergency management.

dailyEditorial.title: Novel Technologies for Identifying Treatment Assignment in a Prehospital Pediatric Emergency Care Clinical Trial
dailyEditorial.takeaway: In the prehospital setting, particular challenges exist in clinical trial design that have a large impact on the quality of the results, including the appropriate and systematic allocation of assigned treatment arm.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Clinical trials are essential for generating evidence-based knowledge to support clinical practice.
dailyEditorial.summary: Clinical trials are essential for generating evidence-based knowledge to support clinical practice. In the prehospital setting, particular challenges exist in clinical trial design that have a large impact on the quality of the results, including the appropriate and systematic allocation of assigned treatment arm.

dailyEditorial.title: Impact of aspirin use on the modified brain injury guidelines for the management of mild traumatic intracranial hemorrhage
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Further prospective studies are necessary to demonstrate that these patients may be safely observed without transfer to a regional trauma center.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: CONCLUSION: We propose that the mBIG1 criteria may be expanded to include patients on pre-injury aspirin.
dailyEditorial.summary: CONCLUSION: We propose that the mBIG1 criteria may be expanded to include patients on pre-injury aspirin. Further prospective studies are necessary to demonstrate that these patients may be safely observed without transfer to a regional trauma center.

dailyEditorial.title: A randomised controlled trial of defibrillation with manual pressure augmentation during out-of-hospital cardiac arrest
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Trial registration ACTRN12621000804886. In this prematurely terminated RCT, MPA reduced transthoracic impedance but did not improve survival or other clinical outcomes in initially shockable OHCA.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: In this prematurely terminated RCT, MPA reduced transthoracic impedance but did not improve survival or other clinical outcomes in initially shockable OHCA.
dailyEditorial.summary: In this prematurely terminated RCT, MPA reduced transthoracic impedance but did not improve survival or other clinical outcomes in initially shockable OHCA. Trial registration ACTRN12621000804886.

dailyEditorial.title: Sequence of advanced airway management and epinephrine administration for paediatric patients with non-shockable out-of-hospital cardiac arrest
dailyEditorial.takeaway: We therefore aimed to investigate the association between the sequence of AAM and epinephrine administration and outcomes in paediatric patients with OHCA with non-shockable rhythms. Methods This retrospective observational study analysed data from a multicentre OHCA registry in Japan from 2014 to 2022.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Background Despite the frequent use of both advanced airway management (AAM) and epinephrine during resuscitation, no studies have evaluated the optimal sequencing of these interventions in paediatric out-of-hospital ca…
dailyEditorial.summary: Background Despite the frequent use of both advanced airway management (AAM) and epinephrine during resuscitation, no studies have evaluated the optimal sequencing of these interventions in paediatric out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA). We therefore aimed to investigate the association between the sequence of AAM and epinephrine administration and outcomes in paediatric patients with OHCA with non-shockable rhythms. Methods This retrospective observational study analysed data from a multicentre OHCA registry in...

dailyEditorial.title: Community emergency medicine: a service review of patients that sustain head injuries in the community--can early intervention by a senior clinical decision maker provide care closer to home?
dailyEditorial.takeaway: 1 It covers 1533 km 2 of rural and urban areas in Southeast Wales, serving a population of 600 000, operating 09:00–17:00 5 days a week with cover on alternate weekends. The team either self-tasks based on call handler information available on the list of waiting 999 calls or responds to requests from ambulance crews.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: The Aneurin Bevan University Health Board’s Physician Response Unit (PRU) comprises a physician-paramedic team that brings senior emergency medicine physician (EMP) (consultant/senior registrar) decision-making to patie…
dailyEditorial.summary: The Aneurin Bevan University Health Board’s Physician Response Unit (PRU) comprises a physician-paramedic team that brings senior emergency medicine physician (EMP) (consultant/senior registrar) decision-making to patients’ homes. 1 It covers 1533 km 2 of rural and urban areas in Southeast Wales, serving a population of 600 000, operating 09:00–17:00 5 days a week with cover on alternate weekends. The team either self-tasks based on call handler information available on the list of waiting 999 calls or responds to...

dailyEditorial.title: Prehospital prediction of clinical course in patients with suspected sepsis: a prospective cohort study
dailyEditorial.takeaway: We examined how variables observed by ambulance personnel predicted the clinical course in patients with suspected sepsis. Methods We prospectively collected prehospital data from patients with suspected sepsis in the ambulance service and supplemented the data with in-hospital findings.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Background Early prediction of the clinical course may aid clinical decisions for patients with suspected sepsis.
dailyEditorial.summary: Background Early prediction of the clinical course may aid clinical decisions for patients with suspected sepsis. We examined how variables observed by ambulance personnel predicted the clinical course in patients with suspected sepsis. Methods We prospectively collected prehospital data from patients with suspected sepsis in the ambulance service and supplemented the data with in-hospital findings.

dailyEditorial.title: Impact of Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation Health Care Workers' Physical Health Parameters on Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation Performance Quality in Simulated Manikin-Based Settings-A Systematic Review
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Achieving adequate depth and sustained frequency of chest compressions requires both strength and endurance among CPR health care workers. CONCLUSION: Physical health and fitness parameters of CPR health care workers are crucial to ensure high-quality CPR.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: CONCLUSION: Physical health and fitness parameters of CPR health care workers are crucial to ensure high-quality CPR.
dailyEditorial.summary: CONCLUSION: Physical health and fitness parameters of CPR health care workers are crucial to ensure high-quality CPR. Achieving adequate depth and sustained frequency of chest compressions requires both strength and endurance among CPR health care workers.

dailyEditorial.title: Association between prehospital time and trauma outcomes: A multicenter cohort study from the Pan-Asian Trauma Outcomes Study (PATOS)
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Prolonged RT and OST were associated with increased mortality, while extended OST and TT were associated with poor functional outcomes. However, these associations may reflect more severely ill patients requiring additional care.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: CONCLUSION: Prehospital time varies across EMS systems.
dailyEditorial.summary: CONCLUSION: Prehospital time varies across EMS systems. Prolonged RT and OST were associated with increased mortality, while extended OST and TT were associated with poor functional outcomes. However, these associations may reflect more severely ill patients requiring additional care.

dailyEditorial.title: A systematic review of mortality and severe morbidities following newborn chest compressions at birth
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Persistent gaps in differentiating DR deaths and stratifying outcomes by gestation, birth setting, care level, and CC timeliness underscore the need for prospective studies aligned with the Neonatal Utstein to improve precision and comparison of evidence in neonatal resuscitation.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: The substantial variation in CC outcome estimates cannot be reliably ascribed to temporal or geographic factors, given the heterogeneity in inclusion criteria.
dailyEditorial.summary: The substantial variation in CC outcome estimates cannot be reliably ascribed to temporal or geographic factors, given the heterogeneity in inclusion criteria. Persistent gaps in differentiating DR deaths and stratifying outcomes by gestation, birth setting, care level, and CC timeliness underscore the need for prospective studies aligned with the Neonatal Utstein to improve precision and comparison of evidence in neonatal resuscitation.

dailyEditorial.title: Endovascular resuscitation: an expert practice review
dailyEditorial.takeaway: The last half-decade has seen landmark trials report on several techniques and adoption is growing despite controversies around risk, efficacy and implementation. As leaders in resuscitation, it is essential that emergency physicians understand the principles and practice of endovascular techniques to advocate for the critically unwell patients that may benefit from EVR and be engaged in future implementation.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Endovascular resuscitation (EVR) describes a collection of novel therapies that aim to bridge patients in shock states or cardiac arrest to definitive treatment, through a combination of haemorrhage control and mechanic…
dailyEditorial.summary: Endovascular resuscitation (EVR) describes a collection of novel therapies that aim to bridge patients in shock states or cardiac arrest to definitive treatment, through a combination of haemorrhage control and mechanical circulatory support. The last half-decade has seen landmark trials report on several techniques and adoption is growing despite controversies around risk, efficacy and implementation. As leaders in resuscitation, it is essential that emergency physicians understand the principles and practice of...

dailyEditorial.title: Barriers to bystander CPR and AED use in out-of-hospital cardiac arrest: A narrative review
dailyEditorial.takeaway: The "chain of survival" framework has served as the foundation of OHCA care for decades. This framework emphasizes early recognition, prompt bystander cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), and rapid defibrillation as critical determinants of outcomes.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) is a leading cause of death worldwide, with a survival to discharge rate of less than 20% in most communities.
dailyEditorial.summary: Out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA) is a leading cause of death worldwide, with a survival to discharge rate of less than 20% in most communities. The "chain of survival" framework has served as the foundation of OHCA care for decades. This framework emphasizes early recognition, prompt bystander cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR), and rapid defibrillation as critical determinants of outcomes.

dailyEditorial.title: EMCrit 424 – 2026 Crashing PE Update
dailyEditorial.takeaway: AHA Guidelines and some new studies strengthen the EMCrit recs for Sick PE management EMCrit Project by Scott Weingart, MD FCCM .
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: AHA Guidelines and some new studies strengthen the EMCrit recs for Sick PE management EMCrit Project by Scott Weingart, MD FCCM .
dailyEditorial.summary: AHA Guidelines and some new studies strengthen the EMCrit recs for Sick PE management EMCrit Project by Scott Weingart, MD FCCM .

dailyEditorial.title: Comparison of three point-of-care ultrasound techniques to confirm endotracheal tube placement: A randomized clinical trial
dailyEditorial.takeaway: CONCLUSION: All three ultrasound techniques demonstrated high diagnostic performance for confirming ETT location after rapid sequence intubation, with TUS providing the shortest confirmation time.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: CONCLUSION: All three ultrasound techniques demonstrated high diagnostic performance for confirming ETT location after rapid sequence intubation, with TUS providing the shortest confirmation time.
dailyEditorial.summary: CONCLUSION: All three ultrasound techniques demonstrated high diagnostic performance for confirming ETT location after rapid sequence intubation, with TUS providing the shortest confirmation time.

dailyEditorial.title: Utilization of emergency departments by individuals with refugee status: a systematic review of studies using data from Canadian care centers
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Although comprehensive data on refugees' use of Canadian emergency departments remains limited, unintentional injuries were the most commonly reported reason for ED visits, with comparatively higher rates of pregnancy-related and mental health-related presentations also observed.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: Although comprehensive data on refugees' use of Canadian emergency departments remains limited, unintentional injuries were the most commonly reported reason for ED visits, with comparatively higher rates of pregnancy-r…
dailyEditorial.summary: Although comprehensive data on refugees' use of Canadian emergency departments remains limited, unintentional injuries were the most commonly reported reason for ED visits, with comparatively higher rates of pregnancy-related and mental health-related presentations also observed.

dailyEditorial.title: Eye-related emergency calls and prehospital management in region Zealand, Danmark: a register-based cohort study
dailyEditorial.takeaway: Several patients classified as eye-related problems were later diagnosed with stroke, highlighting the need for better dispatcher recognition of visual stroke symptoms.
dailyEditorial.shortSummary: CONCLUSION: Eye-related emergency calls were primarily caused by chemical exposures, vision loss, and trauma, with relatively short hospital stays.
dailyEditorial.summary: CONCLUSION: Eye-related emergency calls were primarily caused by chemical exposures, vision loss, and trauma, with relatively short hospital stays. Several patients classified as eye-related problems were later diagnosed with stroke, highlighting the need for better dispatcher recognition of visual stroke symptoms.
